# Questions in Livestream 157

Live Stream #157: The No True Canadian Fallacy

Time links open the YouTube video at the relevant timestamp.

| Time | Question | Short answer / answer direction |
|---:|---|---|
| <a href="https://youtu.be/pqpz1SEv_II?t=483" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">8:03</a> | Did Egyptians view merchants as unscrupulous the way modern people often stereotype businessmen? | He says Egyptians knew merchants could cheat, but trade was so widespread that merchants were not treated as a uniquely suspect class. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/pqpz1SEv_II?t=667" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">11:07</a> | Is Leviticus 13 advanced medical knowledge or just common ancient knowledge? | He says the passage is mostly about ritual impurity management with basic observation rather than unusually advanced medicine. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/pqpz1SEv_II?t=858" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">14:18</a> | Was Osarseph or another early figure ever really called Ramesses? | He says no and spends time dismantling the outdated Ramosis/Ramose to Ramesses confusion used in some apologetic writing. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/pqpz1SEv_II?t=1931" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">32:11</a> | When does the Israelite Sabbath first appear? | He ties it to the manna narrative in Exodus 16 and treats that episode as the first clear Israelite Sabbath observance. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/pqpz1SEv_II?t=2353" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">39:13</a> | Is the mention of Bithiah daughter of Pharaoh in 1 Chronicles 4:18 best treated as original text? | He says it looks like a scribal gloss and criticizes commentators for refusing to say so plainly. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/pqpz1SEv_II?t=2628" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">43:48</a> | How can readers tell when biblical numbers are symbolic and when they are literal? | He says context decides the issue and notes that figures like 7, 12, and 40 often function idiomatically. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/pqpz1SEv_II?t=2977" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">49:37</a> | What did numbers like twelve and seven usually mean? | He says seven often signals perfection and twelve completeness, but warns against overplaying numerology. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/pqpz1SEv_II?t=3324" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">55:24</a> | How triggered does he think Dan McClellan was by his video? | He says he does not think McClellan was personally triggered and frames McClellan as reacting mainly when friends are criticized. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/pqpz1SEv_II?t=3519" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">58:39</a> | What makes someone a scholar? | He says scholarship is fundamentally a level of learning shown by reading and knowing the relevant literature. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/pqpz1SEv_II?t=3858" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:04:18</a> | Is the statement "one cannot prove anything to be 100% certain" self-contradictory? | He calls it self-refuting rather than a strict contradiction, because the universal claim undermines itself. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/pqpz1SEv_II?t=4769" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:19:29</a> | What fallacy is not talked about enough? | He points to misuse of absence-of-evidence arguments, both to deny events and to infer things without evidence. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/pqpz1SEv_II?t=5387" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:29:47</a> | Is Paul's reference to baptism for the dead an example of the tu quoque fallacy? | He says it is not fallacious if Paul is showing that the Corinthians' practice contradicts their denial of resurrection. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/pqpz1SEv_II?t=5804" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:36:44</a> | How do we know the plagues took months? | He dates the Nile-blood plague near the inundation and the barley destruction near March, giving a rough six-month span. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/pqpz1SEv_II?t=6416" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:46:56</a> | Has he read Mike Licona's recent paper on the Chicago Statement? | He says he has not read it, though he offers cautious comments based on Licona's known stance against inerrancy. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/pqpz1SEv_II?t=6518" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:48:38</a> | Does Ephesians 3:8-10 mean believers will preach to angels in heaven? | He says no; he reads it as the church displaying God's wisdom to hostile spiritual powers. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/pqpz1SEv_II?t=6843" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:54:03</a> | Does Ephesians 6:10-12 mean Christians are always directly fighting demons? | He says the struggle is usually indirect, against schemes and influences rather than face-to-face combat with demons. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/pqpz1SEv_II?t=8720" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:25:20</a> | What is a worthless receipt or note from his book? | He explains Deir el-Medina receipts as promissory notes or informal paper currency that became worthless when the debtor died. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/pqpz1SEv_II?t=9000" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:30:00</a> | Does Mark 10:6 teach young-earth creationism? | He says no; the passage is about the foundation and sanctity of marriage, not a young-earth chronology. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/pqpz1SEv_II?t=9269" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:34:29</a> | How common was child sacrifice in Mesopotamia, and was Abraham and Isaac's story unsurprising? | He says child sacrifice is not common in Mesopotamian literature, though some motifs in the Abraham story have Mesopotamian parallels. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/pqpz1SEv_II?t=11154" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:05:54</a> | Are there good PhD schools that major in Egyptology and minor in biblical studies, or the reverse? | He says programs are usually strong in one or the other, and he criticizes some famous options while discussing Egyptology dictionaries. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/pqpz1SEv_II?t=12970" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:36:10</a> | Is it morally wrong to lie to someone trying to find hidden Jews? | He says lying is wrong, but can be justified by a higher moral good such as saving life. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/pqpz1SEv_II?t=13130" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:38:50</a> | If someone self-deletes because of mental illness, is that still sin? | He says mental illness affects responsibility because the person is not thinking clearly, while still grounding forgiveness in Christ. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/pqpz1SEv_II?t=13593" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:46:33</a> | What are his thoughts on hiring editors and effects people for videos? | He warns that many channels hire beyond their means and says creators must treat expenses realistically. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/pqpz1SEv_II?t=14701" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">4:05:01</a> | Is eternal bliss for humans morally fantastical? | He says "bliss" is a caricature and argues that resurrected eternal life is not incoherent if God makes bodies enduring. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/pqpz1SEv_II?t=14991" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">4:09:51</a> | What discovery most enlightened historical context for the Old Testament? | He points to the Merneptah Stele and its implications for Israel as a people group soon after the exodus date he uses. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/pqpz1SEv_II?t=15401" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">4:16:41</a> | Are actual ghosts possible from a Christian perspective? | He says souls of the dead do not linger after death, but other explanations for ghost phenomena remain possible. |
